ads.body: 💸 In a record-breaking domain name purchase, the owner of Crypto.com — co-founder and CEO Kris Marszalek — paid about $70 million in cryptocurrency to acquire the premium domain AI.com. That price eclipses the previous publicly disclosed record of $30 million for Voice.com, making AI.com the most expensive domain sale ever reported. 💲 Marszalek originally bought the Crypto.com domain when his company (then called Monaco) rebranded in 2018, reportedly paying around $12 million for it — a move that helped turn the exchange into one of the world’s biggest crypto platforms. 🤖 Marszalek is building a brand-new consumer AI platform on it. The vision is a personal AI agent service that can do things like send messages, organise tasks, interact with apps and execute actions on behalf of users — essentially an autonomous assistant for everyday digital life. 🏈 The company is planning a high-profile launch tied to a Super Bowl commercial on February 8, 2026, when the platform will start rolling out publicly. Follow @move for interesting stories, amazing places, and incredible people 💫 #Move #AI #Crypto #Domain #Superbowl
ads.title: Move - Interesting stories / Amazing places / Incredible people
ads.page_name: Move - Interesting stories / Amazing places / Incredible people

messaging_angles: Rewards and incentives (missions, diamonds, mystery boxes, crypto rewards)
Wide coin selection and new listings
Large user base social proof (100M+ users)
messaging_analysis.buy_crypto_framing: soft — focuses on rewards, portfolio tools, and convenience rather than direct 'buy now' messaging; buying is positioned as a means to earn rewards or access features
messaging_analysis.fees_messaging: Promotional angle — fees are positioned as a benefit through time-limited offers like '0% credit/debit card fees in first 7 days' rather than avoided entirely
messaging_analysis.simplicity_messaging: Minimal emphasis — only one ad mentions 'registration takes a few minutes'; ease-of-use is implied rather than explicitly highlighted
messaging_analysis.security_messaging: Virtually absent — no direct messaging about security, trust, or safety features in these ads
messaging_analysis.dominant_format: carousel — majority of ads use carousel format, with some images and one video
longest_running_themes: Gamified rewards system (Missions, Diamonds, Mystery Boxes) - 612 days
Large user base social proof - 238 days
messaging_analysis.summary: This competitor relies heavily on incentive-driven marketing with gamification (missions, rewards), card cashback programs, and promotional zero-fee offers to drive engagement. The strategy emphasizes portfolio tools and social proof over direct crypto purchasing, with carousel formats dominating and long-running campaigns focused on rewards and coin variety suggesting these perform best.
